channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Pentagon Identifies Soldier Killed in Iran War as Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton
item.description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, the 17th service member to be named, was killed during the disposal of a drone in northern Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin, Pooja Salhotra and Maia Spoto
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton had been assigned to a unit at Fort Bragg in North Carolina.

item.title: Zelensky Dismisses Ukraine’s Top General After Protests
item.description: The move followed the firing of a popular defense minister, who had clashed with the general over how to fight the war.
item.dc:creator: Marc Santora
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Volodymyr Zelensky and the Ukrainian commander in chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, in Ukraine’s Donetsk region in March.

item.title: Trump Officials Withhold $1 Billion in Medicaid Funds From California and Minnesota Over Suspected Fraud
item.description: The administration accused California and Minnesota of fraudulent activity in their public health insurance programs.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kliff
item.media:description: Dr. Mehmet Oz and Robert F. Kennedy Jr., two top health officials, at a news conference on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Pushes Chinese Election Threat, but Files Show Russia’s Heavy Hand
item.description: Intelligence documents that the White House released to back up President Trump’s claims about China highlight Russian efforts to influence U.S. elections.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer and Dustin Volz
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates
Trump-Ukraine Whistle-Blower Complaint and Impeachment Inquiry
item.media:description: President Trump with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia in 2025. Mr. Trump has consistently resisted admitting U.S. findings that Russia has tried to interfere in American presidential elections.

item.title: Cyclospora Spreads Through Human Feces. How Did It Get Into Our Food Supply?
item.description: The large number of cases suggests that excrement entered the food system on a “surprising scale.”
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan and Caroline Hopkins Legaspi
item.media:description: These cyclospora oocysts were found in a stool sample. The parasite infects the small intestine, causing frequent watery diarrhea that carries its oocysts, or eggs.

item.title: What the Cyclospora Outbreak Reveals
item.description: Warming is expected to fuel the spread of food-borne pathogens. We might not be prepared.
item.dc:creator: Claire Brown
item.media:description: A microscopic view of cyclospora parasite eggs, stained reddish pink, in a lab sample.

item.title: A Family’s Daring Escape From North Korea
item.description: Their resolve for freedom hardened during the pandemic, when they lived through a crippling food shortage and were forced to witness frequent public executions.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.media:credit: Woohae Cho for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kim Yu-mi, right, defected with a group that included her two children and her mother. Life in South Korea has not been quite what they imagined, but they harbor no regrets from deserting the North’s brutal dictatorship and relentless propaganda.

item.title: What Happened When Meta Used A.I. to Ban Accounts on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: Users said the technology mistakenly deleted their accounts. To resolve the issue, they still had to rely on A.I.
item.dc:creator: Stuart A. Thompson and Eli Tan
item.media:credit: Daniel Rodrigues for The New York Times

item.title: OpenAI Says Its A.I. Models Went Rogue and Attacked a Digital Library
item.description: The incident, which targeted the computer systems of another company called Hugging Face, happened while OpenAI was testing the systems.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger
item.media:credit: Aaron Wojack for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI and other companies have warned that their new A.I. systems could be powerful hacking tools.

item.title: Google Releases Three New Gemini A.I. Models
item.description: The models include one that is the company’s most powerful and another that is fine-tuned for cybersecurity, as Google competes with rivals like OpenAI and Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Google campus in Mountain View, Calif.

item.title: How Republicans Turned Olive Garden Into a Voter ID Talking Point
item.description: Republicans claim that Olive Garden polices its Never-Ending Pasta Bowl more tightly than the United States does its elections. It’s not that simple.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Gabby Jones for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Olive Garden in Manhattan. The restaurant chain has recently found itself at the center of a bitter political debate.

item.title: Error Led to 400 Noncitizens Voting in New Jersey, Governor Says
item.description: A 2023 software glitch at the state’s Motor Vehicle Commission led to roughly 6,600 noncitizens being registered to vote during the Murphy administration, Gov. Mikie Sherrill announced.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully
item.media:description: People stand at voting booths during a recent election in New Jersey.

item.title: Troy Jackson Tightens Hold on Democratic Senate Nomination in Maine
item.description: State Democrats released a ballot for their nominating convention on Saturday that includes Mr. Jackson and two long-shot candidates with little public support from delegates.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner
item.media:description: Troy Jackson appears almost certain to emerge as the winner at the Maine Democratic Party’s nominating convention on Saturday in Bangor.

item.title: India’s Young Protesters Are Bloodied but Unbowed
item.description: A day after police forcibly broke up demonstrations, thousands returned to the protest site in New Delhi to keep pushing for action over the country’s failings in education.
item.dc:creator: Pragati K.B., Anupreeta Das and Alex Travelli
item.media:description: Injured protesters at the main protest site in New Delhi on Tuesday. They have vowed to fight on for demands that include the resignation of India’s education minister.

item.title: ‘Too Rare to Care’? A New Center for Rare Diseases Hopes to Change That
item.description: A new nonprofit wants to streamline gene therapy for diseases often avoided by pharmaceutical companies — making treatment more like a routine procedure than a bespoke drug.
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata
item.category: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health
item.media:credit: Moriah Ratner for The New York Times

item.title: The Best Genetic Risk Tools Don’t Work Equally for Everyone
item.description: Genetic prediction models are poised to revolutionize medicine. But because they have been trained on European DNA, they threaten to widen health care disparities.
item.dc:creator: Emily Baumgaertner Nunn

item.title: Inside the Fight to Rid South Africa of Black Immigrants
item.description: As immigration surges in the country, activists are going to extreme lengths to expel foreign nationals, often taking the law into their own hands.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon, Zimasa Matiwane and Joao Silva

item.title: 3 Myths Fueling Anti-Immigrant Sentiment in South Africa
item.description: As the nation convulses with protests and violence, data and research suggest that some of the biggest arguments against foreigners don’t stand up against the facts.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon
item.media:description: Supporters of the March and March movement, one of the main anti-immigrant organizations in South Africa, demonstrating in Johannesburg, in April.

item.title: Restaurant Review: Chez Panisse and Bar Panisse in Berkeley
item.description: The Bay Area farm-to-table classic changed the American restaurant scene. Now it has opened a dynamic new place right next door.
item.dc:creator: Tejal Rao
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times

item.title: Tornado Watch Issued for New York and Other Northeast Areas as Storms Bring Heavy Rain and Winds
item.description: New York was under a tornado watch, and forecasters warned that flash flooding was possible into the evening.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCann, Judson Jones and Nazaneen Ghaffar

item.title: NYC Businesses Brace for More Flooding With Severe Storms in the Forecast
item.description: Some businesses in low-lying areas of New York City have developed routines to keep their goods and customers dry.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard

item.title: Tropical Storm Warnings Issued as Bertha Moves Along the Gulf Coast
item.description: Bertha, the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season, is expected to drop heavy rain in the region this week.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar

item.title: Meet Jimothy, the Seattle Raccoon Who Is Taking Over the Internet
item.description: The stout raccoon, which has a spinal condition, is “an example to all of us that you don’t have to be perfect to be loved,” a Seattle City Council member said.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: Call the Veep, Agent Mulder!
item.description: Have the “space Jews” come to get us at last?
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu and Putin Have All Blundered Into Dead-End Wars
item.description: The quick-fix fantasies of the world’s strongmen.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat

item.title: Mamdani’s Version of DOGE Tries to Put a New Spin on Efficiency
item.description: The mayor’s version has sought to include public input as it formulates changes to the New York City Charter aimed at streamlining the government.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani empaneled a Charter Revision Commission to come up with ways to improve government efficiency.
